gold_change_reason: Added validations for file existence, duplicate flags, shell injection, and conflicting built-in flags. The function now raises exceptions for invalid inputs, preserving core functionality for valid cases.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests for normal, empty, special characters, and multiple params scenarios.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for non-existent input file expecting FileNotFoundError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for duplicate flags expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for shell metacharacters expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for conflicting built-in flags expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include exception tests for adversarial constraints.
implementation_notes: Used os.path.isfile for file existence check.
Defined a set of shell metacharacters to detect command injection.
Duplicate flag detection considers only items starting with '-' and raises ValueError on first repeat.
Conflicting built-in flags are checked against a set of flags that the function adds.
Function logs the constructed command using logging.debug as required.
Preserved the original helper _system_call and logging structure.
Main function is included for completeness but not used by oracle cases.
assumptions: Flags in params are assumed to start with '-' (positive or double dash).
The function assumes the input_fp is a file path (not a directory).
The function does not modify the params list aside from validation.
